title: calylophus drummondiana subsp. berlandieri
intro: Calylophus drummondiana subsp. berlandieri, also known as Drummond's sundrop, is a flowering plant species in the family Calceolariaceae, native to Texas and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Calylophus drummondiana subsp. berlandieri include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
